content.text:
<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 3:23 AM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
oes the discovery-agent on cocierge team have github tools?
</user_query>
I’m checking the `concierge` and `discovery` agent wiring directly to confirm whether GitHub tools are actually attached, not just imported.

No — the `concierge` Discovery member does **not** have GitHub tools wired in right now.

It’s built in `agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py` with:

- `tools=[build_discovery_toolkit()]`

And `build_discovery_toolkit()` in `agents/discovery_tools.py` only exposes `run_discovery_tool` (a local discovery-flow runner), not a GitHub MCP/toolkit.

If you were expecting GitHub tooling, you’re probably thinking of `agents/project_team/discovery_agents.py`, where Planner/ReImaginer are explicitly built with `github_tools`.

<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 3:24 AM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
we need to fix that and give ti repo read tools
</user_query>
I’ll wire the Concierge Discovery member to include a GitHub **read-only** toolkit at construction time, then verify where it’s composed so we don’t accidentally give write capabilities.
I found where Concierge Discovery is built; next I’m checking the existing GitHub tool allowlists so we can reuse a strict **read-only** set instead of inventing one.

I’ve got a concrete fix: add a dedicated **read-only GitHub allowlist** for Concierge Discovery, pass it from the composition root, and keep `run_discovery` intact. I’m applying those edits now and then I’ll run the relevant unit tests.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/agno/agents/concierge/constants.py
@@
 DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE = (
     "Runs the headless repository discovery workflow and returns the link to the generated "
     "artifacts. Route here when the user wants to run discovery on a repository."
 )
 
+# Read-only GitHub tools available to Concierge Discovery for repository inspection.
+CONCIERGE_DISCOVERY_REPO_READ_GITHUB_TOOLS: tuple[str, ...] = (
+    "get_me",
+    "list_branches",
+    "get_file_contents",
+    "search_code",
+    "list_issues",
+    "issue_read",
+    "search_issues",
+    "list_pull_requests",
+    "search_pull_requests",
+    "pull_request_read",
+    "get_commit",
+    "list_commits",
+)
+
 UPDATE_WORKFLOW_STATE_TOOL = "update_workflow_state"
*** End Patch

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/agno/agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py
@@
 from typing import Any
 
 from agno.agent import Agent
 from agno.db.postgres import PostgresDb
+from agno.tools.toolkit import Toolkit
 
 from agents.concierge.constants import DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE, DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID
 from agents.concierge.context import concierge_member_context_kwargs
 from agents.concierge.langfuse_prompts import fetch_discovery_prompt_for_settings
 from agents.concierge.settings import ConciergeSettings, load_concierge_settings
@@
 def build_discovery_agent(
     db: PostgresDb,
     *,
     session_instructions_cache: SessionInstructionsCache,
+    github_repo_read_tools: Toolkit | None = None,
     settings: ConciergeSettings | None = None,
 ) -> Agent:
     resolved = settings or load_concierge_settings()
+    tools: list[Any] = [build_discovery_toolkit()]
+    if github_repo_read_tools is not None:
+        tools.append(github_repo_read_tools)
     return Agent(
         id=DISCOVERY_AGENT_ID,
         name="Discovery",
         role=DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE,
         model=MODEL_MEDIUM,
         db=db,
-        tools=[build_discovery_toolkit()],
+        tools=tools,
         instructions=_discovery_agent_instructions(
             cache=session_instructions_cache,
             settings=resolved,
         ),
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/agno/tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py
@@
 import time
+from types import SimpleNamespace
 from typing import cast
 from unittest.mock import MagicMock
@@
 def test_discovery_agent_has_run_discovery_tool(monkeypatch) -> None:
@@
     assert "run_discovery" in tool_names
 
 
+def test_discovery_agent_includes_repo_read_tools_when_wired(monkeypatch) -> None:
+    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY", "pk-test")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY", "sk-test")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL", "https://langfuse.example.com")
+    cache = _cache_with_discovery(SessionInstructionsCache(max_entries=10))
+    db = cast(PostgresDb, MagicMock())
+    github_toolkit = SimpleNamespace(
+        name="concierge_discovery_github_read_tools",
+        tools=[SimpleNamespace(name="get_file_contents")],
+    )
+    agent = build_discovery_agent(
+        db,
+        session_instructions_cache=cache,
+        github_repo_read_tools=github_toolkit,
+    )
+
+    tool_names: set[str] = set()
+    for toolkit_or_fn in agent.tools or []:
+        for t in getattr(toolkit_or_fn, "tools", []):
+            if name := getattr(t, "name", None):
+                tool_names.add(name)
+
+    assert "run_discovery" in tool_names
+    assert "get_file_contents" in tool_names
+
+
 def test_discovery_agent_instructions_include_workflow_state(monkeypatch) -> None:
*** End Patch

input.command: uv run ruff check agents/concierge/constants.py agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py app/main.py tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py
input.description: Lint changed concierge and main files
Run concierge unit tests in uv env
Show diff for modified concierge files

content.text:
Implemented. Concierge Discovery now has GitHub **repo-read** tools, and I kept it strictly read-only.

### What I changed

- Added a dedicated allowlist in `agents/concierge/constants.py`:
  - `CONCIERGE_DISCOVERY_REPO_READ_GITHUB_TOOLS`
  - Includes read operations like `get_file_contents`, `search_code`, `issue_read`, `pull_request_read`, `list_commits`, etc.
  - Excludes write/mutation tools (`issue_write`, `add_issue_comment`, `create_*`, etc.) on purpose.

- Updated `agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py`:
  - `build_discovery_agent(...)` now accepts optional `github_repo_read_tools`.
  - Agent tools now include:
    - existing `build_discovery_toolkit()` (for `run_discovery`)
    - plus GitHub read toolkit when provided.

- Wired it in composition root `app/main.py`:
  - Builds a scoped MCP toolkit via `build_mcp_tools(...)`
  - Passes `include_tools=list(CONCIERGE_DISCOVERY_REPO_READ_GITHUB_TOOLS)`
  - Injects into `build_discovery_agent(...)` as `github_repo_read_tools`.

- Added unit coverage in `tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py`:
  - New test confirms discovery agent includes both `run_discovery` and a GitHub read tool when wired.

### Verification

- `uv run ruff check agents/concierge/constants.py agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py app/main.py tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py` ✅
- `uv run pytest -q tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py tests/unit/concierge/test_build.py` ✅ (11 passed)

This is the right setup for your ask: Discovery can inspect repos without being able to mutate GitHub state.

<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 3:26 AM (UTC+10)</timestamp>

</user_query>

Yes — the targeted tests passed.

- `uv run pytest -q tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py tests/unit/concierge/test_build.py`
- Result: **11 passed** in ~2s

Also ran lint on changed files:

- `uv run ruff check agents/concierge/constants.py agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py app/main.py tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py`
- Result: **All checks passed**

- Command: `uv run pytest -q`
- Result: **549 passed**

No failures on this run. If you still saw an error, it was likely from a different environment/command (e.g. plain `pytest` outside `uv`, which can miss project deps).
